text.title: Azure Private Link and Private Endpoints Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Azure Private Link and Private Endpoints` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each Private Endpoint MUST have a matching `privatelink.*` Private DNS zone linked to every consumer VNet. Missing link is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without DNS zone link, lookups fall back to public IP silently — PE is bypassed.

rule.statement: Private DNS zone VNet link MUST set `registration_enabled = false`. Auto-registration on PE zones causes duplicate records.
rule.rationale: Auto-registration leaks workload hostnames into Private DNS, polluting the zone.

rule.statement: Target PaaS resource MUST disable public network access (`public_network_access_enabled = false`).
rule.rationale: Leaving public endpoint open defeats the Private Link security model.

rule.statement: Private Endpoint connection MUST be approved on the target resource — `Pending` connections do not route traffic.
rule.rationale: Pending connections silently drop traffic; deploys pass but apps timeout.

rule.statement: Each Azure service requires its specific privatelink zone (e.g. `privatelink.blob.core.windows.net`, `privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net`). Wrong zone is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Wrong zone breaks DNS resolution silently — apps connect to public IPs.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
